folio.label: 172r
folio.l: I am richere & more mygúty , than myne enemys ben , and wel

And wel
knowen ©e , æ by moneye , and by hauynge grete possessiouns ,
ben alle the thynges , of this world , gouäned

¢ And Salamon seitú ;
æ alle thynges obeyen to moneye

¢ Whan Pèdence had herd hirö
housbonde auaunte hi~ of his richesse & of his moneye , desprey-
syngö the power of hise aduäsaries , Spak & seide in this wyse ,
Certes deere Sire I gunte ©ow , æ ©e ben riche & mygúty ,

and æat
the richesses ben goode to he~ , that han wel I geten he~ , & æ wel
konne vsen he~

¢ For rigút as the body of a man , may nat lyuenlyuelyuen
w outen soule , nomoore may it lyue , w outen temporel goo-
des ,
& by richesses may a man gete hi~ grete frendes ,,

And æä-
fore seitú Pamphilles ; I Anatherdes dougúter he seitú be riche ,
she may chese of a thousand men , whicú she wol take to hire
housbonde ,

for of a thousand men , oon wol nat forsaken hire
ne refusen hire

¢ And this Pamphilles seitú also , If æè be rigút
happy , æ is to seyn ; if æè be rigút riche , æè shalt fynden a g-
ret noumbre of felawes & frendes

¢ And if thy fortune chau~-
ge æ æè wexe poore , fare wel frendshipe & felaweshipe ,

For æè
shalt be al allone , w outen any compaignye , but if it be the
compaignye of poore folk

¢ And ©et seitú this Pamphilles more
ouä ; That they æ ben thralle & bonde of lynage , shuln be mad
worthy & noble , by the richesses ,

& rigút so as by richesses , æäe
comen manye goodes , rigút so by pouäte , come there manye
harmes & yueles otiouse dot on the y/ODR/,

for gret pouätee co~streynetú a man to do ma-
nye yueles ,

¢ And æäfore clepetú Cassidore , pouätee , the moder
of ruyne ,

æ is to seyn ; the moder of ouäthrowyngö or fallyngö
doun

¢ And æäfore seith Piers Alfonce ; Oon of the gretteste
aduäsitees of this world , is ,

whan a free man by kynde or of
birthe , is constreyned by pouätee , to eten the almesse of hise
enemy

¢ And æe same seitú Innocent , in oon of hise bookes
he seitú æ sorweful & myshappy is the condicio~n of a poore
beggere ,
for if he axe nat his mete , he dyetú for hungere ,

And
if he axe , he dyetú for shame , & algates necessite co~streynetú
hi~ to aske

¢ And æäfore seitú Salamon ; æ bettre it is to dye
than for to haue swicú pouerte ,

And as the same Salamon
Seitú ; Bettre it is to dye of bitter detú , than for to lyuen in
swicú wyse ,

by theise resons æ I haue seid vn to ©ow , & by
manye othere resou~s æ I koude seye ,

I gunte ©ow , æ riches-
ses ben goode to hem , æ geten he~ wel , and to hem æ wel vsen
tho richesses

¢ And æäfore wol I shewe ©ow , how ©e shuln ha-
ue ©ow , & how ©e shuln bere ©ow , in gaderyngö of ©oure riches-
ses , & in what manäe ©e shuln vsen he~

¢ First ©e shuln ge-
ten he~ , w outen gret desir , by good leyser , sekyngly andCrammed lines?/ODR/
